topic.name: Bach's Cantatas and Musical Forms
topic.description: The documents discuss the compositions of Johann Sebastian Bach, focusing on his church cantatas and their structures, themes, and historical contexts.

topic.name: Insect and Invertebrate Biology
topic.description: The topic encompasses the classification, morphology, and life cycles of various orders of insects and other invertebrates.

Document 1: Hemiptera or true bugs are an order of insects comprising around 50,000 – 80,000 species
Most hemipterans feed on plants, using their sucking and piercing mouthparts to extract plant sap.
Document 2: The external morphology of Lepidoptera is the physiological structure of the bodies of insects belonging to the order Lepidoptera
Lepidopterans undergo complete metamorphosis, going through a four-stage life cycle: egg; larva or caterpillar; pupa or chrysalis; and imago.
Document 3: The flatworms, Platyhelminthes, are a phylum of relatively simple bilaterian, unsegmented, soft-bodied invertebrates
Cestodes (tapeworms) and trematodes (flukes) have complex life-cycles, with mature stages that live as parasites in the digestive systems of fish or land vertebrates.

Document 3: The Schiehallion experiment was an 18th-century experiment to determine the mean density of the Earth.

topic.name: Glacial and River Systems
topic.description: The documents discuss various glacial and river systems, focusing on their geographical characteristics, hydrology, and historical significance.

Document 1: Pine Island Glacier is a large ice stream glacier, and the fastest melting glacier in Antarctica.

Document 2: The River Welland is a lowland river in the east of England, some 65 miles long.

Document 3: Muncy Creek is a tributary of the West Branch Susquehanna River in Pennsylvania.

topic.name: Atmospheric Phenomena and Forecasting
topic.description: This topic encompasses the study of various atmospheric phenomena, including waterspouts and prevailing winds, as well as the methods and technologies used in weather forecasting.

Document 1: Waterspouts are intense columnar vortices that occur over water.
They have a five-part life cycle: formation of a dark spot on the water surface, spiral pattern, formation of a spray ring, development of the visible condensation funnel, and ultimately decay.
Document 2: Prevailing winds are winds that blow predominantly from a single general direction over a particular point on the Earth's surface.
Knowledge of the prevailing wind allows the development of prevention strategies for wind erosion of agricultural land.
Document 3: Weather forecasting is the application of science and technology to predict the state of the atmosphere for a given location.
Weather forecasts are made by collecting quantitative data about the current state of the atmosphere.

topic.name: African American History in the South
topic.description: This topic encompasses the historical experiences and contributions of African Americans in southern regions of the United States, particularly in relation to migration, settlement, and cultural development.

Document 1: African Americans in Omaha, Nebraska are central to the development and growth of the 43rd largest city in the United States.
Omaha was the city on the Plains that attracted the most African-American migrants from the South in the Great Migration of the early 20th century.
Document 2: Simon Favre was an interpreter of the Muskogean languages, particularly Choctaw and Chickasaw, for the French, British, Spanish and Americans in the part of West Florida.
He became well versed in the language and culture of the Choctaws, and was involved with several treaties between the Europeans and natives.
Document 3: Spanish Texas was one of the interior provinces of the Spanish colonial Viceroyalty of New Spain from 1690 until 1821.

'Til It Happens to You' was placed during two sequences in the film, highlighting the pain of sexual assault.

topic.name: Australian Cricket History
topic.description: The documents discuss notable Australian cricketers and their contributions to the sport during different eras.

Document 1: Bill Johnston was a key member of Donald Bradman 's famous Australian cricket team
The Australians went undefeated in their 34 matches during the English summer
Document 2: Bob Willis is an English former cricketer who played for Surrey, Warwickshire, Northern Transvaal and England
Willis spearheaded several England bowling attacks between 1971 and 1984
Document 3: Joe Darling was an Australian cricketer who played 34 Test matches
He was captain of the Australian cricket team in England in 1902

topic.name: Australian and New Zealand Military Contributions in WWI
topic.description: This topic covers the military involvement of Australian and New Zealand forces during World War I, including key campaigns and regiments.

Document 1: the outbreak of World War I was greeted with considerable enthusiasm
the Australians and New Zealanders, grouped together as the Australian and New Zealand Army Corps (ANZAC)
Document 2: the Canterbury Mounted Rifles Regiment was a mounted infantry regiment from New Zealand
They participated in the largest battle of that theatre at Chunuk Bair
Document 3: the 15th Battalion was an infantry battalion of the Australian Army
the battalion fought during the Gallipoli Campaign and on the Western Front during the First World War
